claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their names and ages

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have two kids - Alex, who's 17, and Blake, who's 3 years old. They both live with me here in the UK. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have Alex (17 years old) and Blake (3 years old), and they both live with you in the UK.

Let me ask you a few questions to assess eligibility for each child:

**Is Alex (your 17-year-old) currently in full-time approved education or training?** 

This is important because children aged 16 or 17 may still qualify for Child Benefit if they're in approved education or enrolled in certain training programs.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and isn't doing anything like that right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that about Alex.

Now let me ask about your younger child:

**Is Blake (your 3-year-old) currently in hospital or in care (such as local authority care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in hospital or care. He lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more important question:

**Are you currently living or working in the UK?** This helps confirm you meet the residence requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm based here with both my children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Let me ask one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake, or anyone else who has the children living with them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else claiming for them. They both live with me and nobody else is getting Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment:

## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

**For Blake (3 years old): ✓ ELIGIBLE**
- Blake is under 16 years old and lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- Blake is not in hospital or care
- You meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Blake

**For Alex (17 years old): ✗ NOT ELIGIBLE**
- Alex is 17, which means he's in the 16-17 age group
- For children aged 16 or 17, Child Benefit can only be claimed if they are in approved full-time education or enrolled in approved training
- Since Alex is not currently in education or training, you cannot claim Child Benefit for him

## What This Means

You **can claim Child Benefit for Blake**, but **not for Alex** at this time. 

However, if Alex's situation changes and he enrolls in approved full-time education or training in the future, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit for him again (until he turns 20 or leaves education/training, whichever comes first).

Would you like any clarification about this assessment or information about how to make a claim for Blake?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is not in full-time approved education or training. Child Benefit can only be claimed for children aged 16 or 17 if they are in approved education or enrolled in approved training. Since Alex is not meeting this requirement, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 3 years old, which is under the age threshold of 16. Blake lives with you in the UK, is not in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. You meet the responsibility requirements as Blake lives with you. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with two children: Alex (17) and Blake (3). Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility is assessed per child. Blake qualifies as he is under 16 and lives with the claimant. Alex does not qualify because, although he is under 20, he is 16 or over and not in approved education or training, which is a requirement for children in that age group.

Child Benefit can only be claimed for children aged 16 or 17 if they are in approved education or enrolled in approved training. Since Alex is not meeting this requirement, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 3 years old, which is under the age threshold of 16. Blake lives with you in the UK, is not in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. You meet the responsibility requirements as Blake lives with you. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with two children: Alex (17) and Blake (3). Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility is assessed per child. Blake qualifies as he is under 16 and lives with the claimant. Alex does not qualify because, although he is under 20, he is 16 or over and not in approved education or training, which is a requirement for children in that age group.
